== Events == * {{ill|Anwa|ja|安和|12=draft}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} [[969]] {{ill|April 14|ja|4月14日}}) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} [[970]] {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Anwa|ja|安和|12=draft}} to {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄|12=draft}}. * ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} [[1356]] {{ill|April 26|ja|4月26日}}) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the {{ill|Utsubunashū|ja|菟玖波集}}. * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} [[1826]] {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日}}) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} [[1870]] {{ill|April 25|ja|4月25日}}) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} [[850]] {{ill|May 10|ja|5月10日}}) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[880]]). * [[Keichō]] 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} [[1604]] {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日}}) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[1648]]). * {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} [[1844]] {{ill|May 12|ja|5月12日}}) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[1870]]). * 明治2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} [[1869]] {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日}}) – {{ill|Inoue Junosuke|ja|井上準之助}}, a politician (died [[1932]]). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} [[1872]] {{ill|May 2|ja|5月2日}}) – {{ill|Higuchi Ichiyō|ja|樋口一葉}}, a novelist and waka poet (died [[1896]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft}} 5th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} [[649]] {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日}}) – {{ill|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂}}, a noble and grandson of {{ill|Soga Umako|ja|蘇我馬子}}. * {{ill|Meio|ja|明応|12=draft}} 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} [[1499]] {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日}}) – {{ill|Rennyo|ja|蓮如}}, the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[1415]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|24|3|26|2|25|4|3|0325}}
[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月廿五]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月廿五]] [[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月廿五]]

== Events == * {{ill|Anwa|ja|安和|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[969]] {{ill|April 14|ja|4月14日|12=draft}}) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=draft}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[970]] {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日|12=draft}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Anwa|ja|安和|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄|12=draft|12=draft}}. * ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[1356]] {{ill|April 26|ja|4月26日|12=draft}}) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the {{ill|Utsubunashū|ja|菟玖波集|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1826]] {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=draft}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1870]] {{ill|April 25|ja|4月25日|12=draft}}) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[850]] {{ill|May 10|ja|5月10日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|12=draft}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[880]]). * [[Keichō]] 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1604]] {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|12=draft}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[1648]]). * {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft|12=draft}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1844]] {{ill|May 12|ja|5月12日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|12=draft}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[1870]]). * 明治2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1869]] {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Inoue Junosuke|ja|井上準之助|12=draft}}, a politician (died [[1932]]). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1872]] {{ill|May 2|ja|5月2日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Higuchi Ichiyō|ja|樋口一葉|12=draft}}, a novelist and waka poet (died [[1896]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft|12=draft}} 5th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[649]] {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|12=draft}}, a noble and grandson of {{ill|Soga Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Meio|ja|明応|12=draft|12=draft}} 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[1499]] {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Rennyo|ja|蓮如|12=draft}}, the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[1415]]).

[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:三月廿五]] {{Lunisolar March}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March|12=draft|12=draft}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Anwa|ja|安和|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[969]] {{ill|April 14|ja|4月14日|12=draft}}) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=draft}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄|12=draft|12=draft}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[970]] {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日|12=draft}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Anwa|ja|安和|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄|12=draft|12=draft}}. * ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[1356]] {{ill|April 26|ja|4月26日|12=draft}}) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the {{ill|Utsubunashū|ja|菟玖波集|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1826]] {{ill|May 1|ja|5月1日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=draft}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1870]] {{ill|April 25|ja|4月25日|12=draft}}) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * {{ill|Kashō|ja|嘉祥|12=draft|12=draft}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[850]] {{ill|May 10|ja|5月10日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|12=draft}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[880]]). * [[Keichō]] 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1604]] {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|12=draft}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[1648]]). * {{ill|Tenpō|ja|天保|12=draft|12=draft}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1844]] {{ill|May 12|ja|5月12日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|12=draft}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[1870]]). * 明治2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1869]] {{ill|May 6|ja|5月6日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Inoue Junosuke|ja|井上準之助|12=draft}}, a politician (died [[1932]]). * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1872]] {{ill|May 2|ja|5月2日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Higuchi Ichiyō|ja|樋口一葉|12=draft}}, a novelist and waka poet (died [[1896]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Taika|ja|大化|12=draft|12=draft}} 5th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[649]] {{ill|May 11|ja|5月11日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro|ja|蘇我倉山田石川麻呂|12=draft}}, a noble and grandson of {{ill|Soga Umako|ja|蘇我馬子|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Meio|ja|明応|12=draft|12=draft}} 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[1499]] {{ill|May 5|ja|5月5日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Rennyo|ja|蓮如|12=draft}}, the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[1415]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|24|3|26|2|25|4|3|0325}}

revision.text:
{{旧暦3月}} '''旧暦3月25日'''は[[3月 (旧暦)|旧暦3月]]の25日目である。六曜は[[六曜#先負|先負]]である。
== できごと == * [[安和]]2年（[[ユリウス暦]][[969年]][[4月14日]]） - [[安和の変]]。源満仲の密告により源高明が皇太子守平親王廃立を[[陰謀]]したとして[[大宰権帥]]に左遷。藤原氏政権が確立 * [[天禄]]元年（ユリウス暦[[970年]][[5月3日]]） - [[安和]]より天禄に[[改元]] * （ユリウス暦[[1356年]][[4月26日]]） - 二条良基が日本初の連歌選集『[[菟玖波集]]』を編纂 * [[文政]]9年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1826年]][[5月1日]]） - [[フィリップ・フランツ・フォン・シーボルト|シーボルト]]が[[オランダ商館]]長（[[カピタン]]）に随行して[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]・[[徳川家斉]]に謁見 * [[明治]]3年（グレゴリオ暦[[1870年]][[4月25日]]） - 東京～神奈川間の鉄道の測量を開始
== 誕生日 == * [[嘉祥]]3年（ユリウス暦[[850年]][[5月10日]]） - [[清和天皇]]、56代[[天皇]]（+ [[880年]]） * [[慶長]]9年（グレゴリオ暦[[1604年]][[4月24日]]） - [[松平直基]]、[[越前勝山藩]]主・[[大野藩]]主・[[山形藩]]主・[[姫路藩]]主（+ [[1648年]]） * [[天保]]15年（グレゴリオ暦[[1844年]][[5月12日]]） - [[雲井龍雄]]、[[米沢藩]]士・[[政治家]]（+ [[1870年]]） * 明治2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1869年]][[5月6日]]） - [[井上準之助]]、政治家（+ [[1932年]]） * [[明治]]5年（グレゴリオ暦[[1872年]][[5月2日]]） - [[樋口一葉]]、[[小説家]]・[[歌人]]（+ [[1896年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[大化]]5年（ユリウス暦[[649年]][[5月11日]]） - [[蘇我倉山田石川麻呂]]、[[豪族]]・[[蘇我馬子]]の孫 * [[明応]]8年（ユリウス暦[[1499年]][[5月5日]]） - [[蓮如]]、[[浄土真宗]]中興の祖（* [[1415年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|3|24|3|26|2|25|4|3|0325}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[3月25日 (旧暦)]] to [[March 25 (Lunisolar)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{旧暦3月}} '''旧暦3月25日'''は[[3月 (旧暦)|旧暦3月]]の25日目である。六曜は[[六曜#先負|先負]]である。
== できごと == * [[安和]]2年（[[ユリウス暦]][[969年]][[4月14日]]） - [[安和の変]]。源満仲の密告により源高明が皇太子守平親王廃立を[[陰謀]]したとして[[大宰権帥]]に左遷。藤原氏政権が確立 * [[天禄]]元年（ユリウス暦[[970年]][[5月3日]]） - [[安和]]より天禄に[[改元]] * （ユリウス暦[[1356年]][[4月26日]]） - 二条良基が日本初の連歌選集『[[菟玖波集]]』を編纂 * [[文政]]9年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1826年]][[5月1日]]） - [[フィリップ・フランツ・フォン・シーボルト|シーボルト]]が[[オランダ商館]]長（[[カピタン]]）に随行して[[征夷大将軍|将軍]]・[[徳川家斉]]に謁見 * [[明治]]3年（グレゴリオ暦[[1870年]][[4月25日]]） - 東京～神奈川間の鉄道の測量を開始
== 誕生日 == * [[嘉祥]]3年（ユリウス暦[[850年]][[5月10日]]） - [[清和天皇]]、56代[[天皇]]（+ [[880年]]） * [[慶長]]9年（グレゴリオ暦[[1604年]][[4月24日]]） - [[松平直基]]、[[越前勝山藩]]主・[[大野藩]]主・[[山形藩]]主・[[姫路藩]]主（+ [[1648年]]） * [[天保]]15年（グレゴリオ暦[[1844年]][[5月12日]]） - [[雲井龍雄]]、[[米沢藩]]士・[[政治家]]（+ [[1870年]]） * 明治2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1869年]][[5月6日]]） - [[井上準之助]]、政治家（+ [[1932年]]） * [[明治]]5年（グレゴリオ暦[[1872年]][[5月2日]]） - [[樋口一葉]]、[[小説家]]・[[歌人]]（+ [[1896年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[大化]]5年（ユリウス暦[[649年]][[5月11日]]） - [[蘇我倉山田石川麻呂]]、[[豪族]]・[[蘇我馬子]]の孫 * [[明応]]8年（ユリウス暦[[1499年]][[5月5日]]） - [[蓮如]]、[[浄土真宗]]中興の祖（* [[1415年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|3|24|3|26|2|25|4|3|0325}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 25일]][[zh_min_nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月廿五]][[zh:三月廿五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q839016]] Wikidata name: 25th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 25|qq=March 25}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|qid=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:969|969]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|qq=|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:970|970]] [[:en:May 3|May 3]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] to [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]]. * ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1356|1356]] [[:en:April 26|April 26]]) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the [[:en:Tsukubashū|Utsubunashū]]. * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1826|1826]] [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|qq=|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:850|850]] [[:en:May 10|May 10]]) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:880|880]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|qq=|1=Matsudaira Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5324923}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[:en:1648|1648]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1844|1844]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|qq=|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1870|1870]]). * 明治2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – [[:en:Junnosuke Inoue|Inoue Junosuke]], a politician (died [[:en:1932|1932]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:May 2|May 2]]) – [[:en:Ichiyō Higuchi|Higuchi Ichiyō]], a novelist and waka poet (died [[:en:1896|1896]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:649|649]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]]) – [[:en:Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro]], a noble and grandson of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga Umako]]. * [[:en:Meiō|Meio]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1499|1499]] [[:en:May 5|May 5]]) – [[:en:Rennyo|Rennyo]], the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[:en:1415|1415]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|24|3|26|2|25|4|3|0325|qq=}}

Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|qid=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:969|969]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|qq=|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:970|970]] [[:en:May 3|May 3]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] to [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]]. * ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1356|1356]] [[:en:April 26|April 26]]) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the [[:en:Tsukubashū|Utsubunashū]]. * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1826|1826]] [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|qq=|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:850|850]] [[:en:May 10|May 10]]) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:880|880]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|qq=|1=Matsudaira Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5324923}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[:en:1648|1648]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1844|1844]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|qq=|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1870|1870]]). * 明治2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – [[:en:Junnosuke Inoue|Inoue Junosuke]], a politician (died [[:en:1932|1932]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:May 2|May 2]]) – [[:en:Ichiyō Higuchi|Higuchi Ichiyō]], a novelist and waka poet (died [[:en:1896|1896]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:649|649]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]]) – [[:en:Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro]], a noble and grandson of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga Umako]]. * [[:en:Meiō|Meio]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1499|1499]] [[:en:May 5|May 5]]) – [[:en:Rennyo|Rennyo]], the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[:en:1415|1415]]).

== Events == * [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:969|969]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|qq=|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:970|970]] [[:en:May 3|May 3]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] to [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]]. * ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1356|1356]] [[:en:April 26|April 26]]) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the [[:en:Tsukubashū|Utsubunashū]]. * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1826|1826]] [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|qq=|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:850|850]] [[:en:May 10|May 10]]) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:880|880]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|qq=|1=Matsudaira Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5324923}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[:en:1648|1648]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1844|1844]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|qq=|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1870|1870]]). * 明治2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – [[:en:Junnosuke Inoue|Inoue Junosuke]], a politician (died [[:en:1932|1932]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:May 2|May 2]]) – [[:en:Ichiyō Higuchi|Higuchi Ichiyō]], a novelist and waka poet (died [[:en:1896|1896]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:649|649]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]]) – [[:en:Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro]], a noble and grandson of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga Umako]]. * [[:en:Meiō|Meio]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1499|1499]] [[:en:May 5|May 5]]) – [[:en:Rennyo|Rennyo]], the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[:en:1415|1415]]).

Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:969|969]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|qq=|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:970|970]] [[:en:May 3|May 3]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] to [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]]. * ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1356|1356]] [[:en:April 26|April 26]]) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the [[:en:Tsukubashū|Utsubunashū]]. * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1826|1826]] [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|qq=|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:850|850]] [[:en:May 10|May 10]]) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:880|880]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|qq=|1=Matsudaira Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5324923}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[:en:1648|1648]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1844|1844]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|qq=|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1870|1870]]). * 明治2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – [[:en:Junnosuke Inoue|Inoue Junosuke]], a politician (died [[:en:1932|1932]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:May 2|May 2]]) – [[:en:Ichiyō Higuchi|Higuchi Ichiyō]], a novelist and waka poet (died [[:en:1896|1896]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:649|649]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]]) – [[:en:Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro]], a noble and grandson of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga Umako]]. * [[:en:Meiō|Meio]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1499|1499]] [[:en:May 5|May 5]]) – [[:en:Rennyo|Rennyo]], the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[:en:1415|1415]]).

Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:969|969]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:970|970]] [[:en:May 3|May 3]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] to [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]]. * ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1356|1356]] [[:en:April 26|April 26]]) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the [[:en:Tsukubashū|Utsubunashū]]. * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1826|1826]] [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:850|850]] [[:en:May 10|May 10]]) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:880|880]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|1=Matsudaira Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5324923}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[:en:1648|1648]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1844|1844]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1870|1870]]). * 明治2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – [[:en:Junnosuke Inoue|Inoue Junosuke]], a politician (died [[:en:1932|1932]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:May 2|May 2]]) – [[:en:Ichiyō Higuchi|Higuchi Ichiyō]], a novelist and waka poet (died [[:en:1896|1896]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:649|649]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]]) – [[:en:Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro]], a noble and grandson of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga Umako]]. * [[:en:Meiō|Meio]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1499|1499]] [[:en:May 5|May 5]]) – [[:en:Rennyo|Rennyo]], the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[:en:1415|1415]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|24|3|26|2|25|4|3|0325|qq=}}

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:969|969]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:970|970]] [[:en:May 3|May 3]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] to [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]]. * ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1356|1356]] [[:en:April 26|April 26]]) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the [[:en:Tsukubashū|Utsubunashū]]. * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1826|1826]] [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:850|850]] [[:en:May 10|May 10]]) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:880|880]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|1=Matsudaira Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5324923}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[:en:1648|1648]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1844|1844]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1870|1870]]). * 明治2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – [[:en:Junnosuke Inoue|Inoue Junosuke]], a politician (died [[:en:1932|1932]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:May 2|May 2]]) – [[:en:Ichiyō Higuchi|Higuchi Ichiyō]], a novelist and waka poet (died [[:en:1896|1896]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:649|649]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]]) – [[:en:Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro]], a noble and grandson of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga Umako]]. * [[:en:Meiō|Meio]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1499|1499]] [[:en:May 5|May 5]]) – [[:en:Rennyo|Rennyo]], the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[:en:1415|1415]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|24|3|26|2|25|4|3|0325|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:969|969]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:970|970]] [[:en:May 3|May 3]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] to [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]]. * ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1356|1356]] [[:en:April 26|April 26]]) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the [[:en:Tsukubashū|Utsubunashū]]. * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1826|1826]] [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:850|850]] [[:en:May 10|May 10]]) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:880|880]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|1=Matsudaira Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5324923}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[:en:1648|1648]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1844|1844]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1870|1870]]). * 明治2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – [[:en:Junnosuke Inoue|Inoue Junosuke]], a politician (died [[:en:1932|1932]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:May 2|May 2]]) – [[:en:Ichiyō Higuchi|Higuchi Ichiyō]], a novelist and waka poet (died [[:en:1896|1896]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:649|649]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]]) – [[:en:Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro]], a noble and grandson of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga Umako]]. * [[:en:Meiō|Meio]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1499|1499]] [[:en:May 5|May 5]]) – [[:en:Rennyo|Rennyo]], the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[:en:1415|1415]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|24|3|26|2|25|4|3|0325|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月廿五]][[zh:三月廿五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839016]] Wikidata name: 25th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 25|qq=March 25}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:969|969]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:970|970]] [[:en:May 3|May 3]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] to [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]]. * ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1356|1356]] [[:en:April 26|April 26]]) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the [[:en:Tsukubashū|Utsubunashū]]. * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1826|1826]] [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:850|850]] [[:en:May 10|May 10]]) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:880|880]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|1=Matsudaira Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5324923}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[:en:1648|1648]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1844|1844]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1870|1870]]). * 明治2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – [[:en:Junnosuke Inoue|Inoue Junosuke]], a politician (died [[:en:1932|1932]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:May 2|May 2]]) – [[:en:Ichiyō Higuchi|Higuchi Ichiyō]], a novelist and waka poet (died [[:en:1896|1896]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:649|649]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]]) – [[:en:Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro]], a noble and grandson of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga Umako]]. * [[:en:Meiō|Meio]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1499|1499]] [[:en:May 5|May 5]]) – [[:en:Rennyo|Rennyo]], the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[:en:1415|1415]]).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 25일]][[zh_min_nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月廿五]][[zh:三月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 25일]][[zh_min_nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月廿五]][[zh:三月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月廿五]][[zh:三月廿五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839016]] Wikidata name: 25th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 25|qq=March 25}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:969|969]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:970|970]] [[:en:May 3|May 3]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] to [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]]. * ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1356|1356]] [[:en:April 26|April 26]]) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the [[:en:Tsukubashū|Utsubunashū]]. * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1826|1826]] [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:850|850]] [[:en:May 10|May 10]]) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:880|880]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|1=Matsudaira Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5324923}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[:en:1648|1648]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1844|1844]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1870|1870]]). * 明治2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – [[:en:Junnosuke Inoue|Inoue Junosuke]], a politician (died [[:en:1932|1932]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:May 2|May 2]]) – [[:en:Ichiyō Higuchi|Higuchi Ichiyō]], a novelist and waka poet (died [[:en:1896|1896]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:649|649]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]]) – [[:en:Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro]], a noble and grandson of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga Umako]]. * [[:en:Meiō|Meio]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1499|1499]] [[:en:May 5|May 5]]) – [[:en:Rennyo|Rennyo]], the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[:en:1415|1415]]).
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|3|24|3|26|2|25|4|3|0325|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:969|969]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:970|970]] [[:en:May 3|May 3]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] to [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]]. * ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1356|1356]] [[:en:April 26|April 26]]) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the [[:en:Tsukubashū|Utsubunashū]]. * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1826|1826]] [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:850|850]] [[:en:May 10|May 10]]) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:880|880]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|1=Matsudaira Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5324923}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[:en:1648|1648]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1844|1844]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1870|1870]]). * 明治2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – [[:en:Junnosuke Inoue|Inoue Junosuke]], a politician (died [[:en:1932|1932]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:May 2|May 2]]) – [[:en:Ichiyō Higuchi|Higuchi Ichiyō]], a novelist and waka poet (died [[:en:1896|1896]]).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 25일]][[zh_min_nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月廿五]][[zh:三月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 25일]][[zh_min_nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:三月廿五]][[zh:三月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月廿五]][[zh:三月廿五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839016]] Wikidata name: 25th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 25|qq=March 25}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] 2nd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:969|969]] [[:en:April 14|April 14]]) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 1st year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:970|970]] [[:en:May 3|May 3]]) – The era name was changed from [[:en:Anna (era)|Anwa]] to [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]]. * ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1356|1356]] [[:en:April 26|April 26]]) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the [[:en:Tsukubashū|Utsubunashū]]. * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1826|1826]] [[:en:May 1|May 1]]) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 3rd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1870|1870]] [[:en:April 25|April 25]]) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * [[:en:Kashō|Kashō]] 3rd year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:850|850]] [[:en:May 10|May 10]]) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:880|880]]). * [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] 9th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1604|1604]] [[:en:April 24|April 24]]) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|1=Matsudaira Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5324923}}, lord of the Echizen, Ōno, Yamagata, and Himeji Domains (died [[:en:1648|1648]]). * [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 15th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1844|1844]] [[:en:May 12|May 12]]) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died [[:en:1870|1870]]). * 明治2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1869|1869]] [[:en:May 6|May 6]]) – [[:en:Junnosuke Inoue|Inoue Junosuke]], a politician (died [[:en:1932|1932]]). * [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:May 2|May 2]]) – [[:en:Ichiyō Higuchi|Higuchi Ichiyō]], a novelist and waka poet (died [[:en:1896|1896]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Taika (era)|Taika]] 5th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:649|649]] [[:en:May 11|May 11]]) – [[:en:Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro]], a noble and grandson of [[:en:Soga no Umako|Soga Umako]]. * [[:en:Meiō|Meio]] 8th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1499|1499]] [[:en:May 5|May 5]]) – [[:en:Rennyo|Rennyo]], the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born [[:en:1415|1415]]).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月廿五]][[zh:三月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月廿五]][[zh:三月廿五]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:3 nguŏk 25 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:3月25日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 3월 25일]][[zh-min-nan:3 goe̍h 25 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:三月廿五]][[zh:三月廿五]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839016]] Wikidata name: 25th day of the 3rd month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|March 25|qq=March 25}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Anna (era)|en|Anna (era)|WD=Q1373003|lt=Anwa}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|969|en|969|WD=Q23708|lt=969}} {{ill|April 14|en|April 14|WD=Q2500|lt=April 14}}) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|970|en|970|WD=Q24047|lt=970}} {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|WD=Q2547|lt=May 3}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Anna (era)|en|Anna (era)|WD=Q1373003|lt=Anwa}} to {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku}}. * ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1356|en|1356|WD=Q6130|lt=1356}} {{ill|April 26|en|April 26|WD=Q2532|lt=April 26}}) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the {{ill|Tsukubashū|en|Tsukubashū|WD=Q3007417|lt=Utsubunashū}}. * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1826|en|1826|WD=Q7004|lt=1826}} {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1}}) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}} {{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25}}) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
== Births == * {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō}} 3rd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|850|en|850|WD=Q30364|lt=850}} {{ill|May 10|en|May 10|WD=Q2557|lt=May 10}}) – {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|ja|正和天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seiwa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 56th Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1604|en|1604|WD=Q6738|lt=1604}} {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|WD=Q2529|lt=April 24}}) – {{ill|Matsudaira Naomoto|ja|松平直基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Naomoto|1=Matsudaira {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 15th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1844|en|1844|WD=Q7633|lt=1844}} {{ill|May 12|en|May 12|WD=Q2559|lt=May 12}}) – {{ill|Unii Ryūo|ja|雲井龍雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unii Ryūo|1=Tatsuo Kumoi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11660002}}, a military official and politician from the Yonezawa Domain (died {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}}). * 明治2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}} {{ill|May 6|en|May 6|WD=Q2552|lt=May 6}}) – {{ill|Junnosuke Inoue|en|Junnosuke Inoue|WD=Q5385606|lt=Inoue Junosuke}}, a politician (died {{ill|1932|en|1932|WD=Q18743|lt=1932}}). * {{ill|Meiji 2|WD=Q2545|lt=May 2}}) – {{ill|Ichiyō Higuchi|en|Ichiyō Higuchi|WD=Q237948|lt=Higuchi Ichiyō}}, a novelist and waka poet (died {{ill|1896|en|1896|WD=Q7846|lt=1896}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Taika (era)|en|Taika (era)|WD=Q1077930|lt=Taika}} 5th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|649|en|649|WD=Q31117|lt=649}} {{ill|May 11|en|May 11|WD=Q2558|lt=May 11}}) – {{ill|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|en|Soga no Kurayamada no Ishikawa no Maro|WD=Q3488899|lt=Soga Kurayamada Ishikawa Maro}}, a noble and grandson of {{ill|Soga no Umako|en|Soga no Umako|WD=Q1191233|lt=Soga Umako}}. * {{ill|Meiō|en|Meiō|WD=Q1193841|lt=Meio}} 8th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1499|en|1499|WD=Q6708|lt=1499}} {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}}) – {{ill|Rennyo|en|Rennyo|WD=Q323600|lt=Rennyo}}, the founder of Jōdo Shinshū (born {{ill|1415|en|1415|WD=Q6183|lt=1415}}).

== Events == * {{ill|Anna (era)|en|Anna (era)|WD=Q1373003|lt=Anwa}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|969|en|969|WD=Q23708|lt=969}} {{ill|April 14|en|April 14|WD=Q2500|lt=April 14}}) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|1=Anwa no Hen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|970|en|970|WD=Q24047|lt=970}} {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|WD=Q2547|lt=May 3}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Anna (era)|en|Anna (era)|WD=Q1373003|lt=Anwa}} to {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku}}. * ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1356|en|1356|WD=Q6130|lt=1356}} {{ill|April 26|en|April 26|WD=Q2532|lt=April 26}}) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the {{ill|Tsukubashū|en|Tsukubashū|WD=Q3007417|lt=Utsubunashū}}. * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1826|en|1826|WD=Q7004|lt=1826}} {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|WD=Q2544|lt=May 1}}) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870}} {{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25}}) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.

{{Not to be confused with|March 25|qq=March 25}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=三月廿五|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 3월 25일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar March|qq=}} '''March 25''' is the 25th day of the month {{ill|March (Lunisolar)|ja|3月 (旧暦)|lt=March (Yayoi)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 3rd month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|qid=Q96759842|1=senpu}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Anna (era)|en|Anna (era)|qid=Q1373003|lt=Anwa|1=Anna (era)}} 2nd year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|969|en|969|qid=Q23708|lt=969|1=969}} {{ill|April 14|en|April 14|qid=Q2500|lt=April 14|1=April 14}}) – In the incident known as {{ill|Anwa no Hen|ja|安和の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anwa no Hen|1=Q11450437|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11450437}}, due to a denunciation by Genmanaka, 【Minamoto Takamasa】 (源高明) conspired to depose Crown Prince Morihei (守平親王) and was demoted to the Rank of 【Dazaiken-sui|大宰権帥】; this marked the establishment of the Fujiwara regency. * {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|qid=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku|1=Tenroku}} 1st year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|970|en|970|qid=Q24047|lt=970|1=970}} {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|qid=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}}) – The era name was changed from {{ill|Anna (era)|en|Anna (era)|qid=Q1373003|lt=Anwa|1=Anna (era)}} to {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|qid=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku|1=Tenroku}}. * ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1356|en|1356|qid=Q6130|lt=1356|1=1356}} {{ill|April 26|en|April 26|qid=Q2532|lt=April 26|1=April 26}}) – Nijō Yoshimoto compiled Japan’s first renga anthology, the {{ill|Tsukubashū|en|Tsukubashū|qid=Q3007417|lt=Utsubunashū|1=Tsukubashū}}. * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|qid=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 9th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1826|en|1826|qid=Q7004|lt=1826|1=1826}} {{ill|May 1|en|May 1|qid=Q2544|lt=May 1|1=May 1}}) – {{ill|Philip Franz von Siebold|ja|シーボルト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Philip Franz von Siebold|1=Siebold|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1961155}}, while accompanying the head of the Dutch trading post, attempted to export Japanese maps and other prohibited items, and was subsequently expelled. * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 3rd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1870|en|1870|qid=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}} {{ill|April 25|en|April 25|qid=Q2531|lt=April 25|1=April 25}}) – Surveying for Japan’s first railway between Tokyo and Kanagawa began.
